  • Build the certification-badge rendering slot in line with the Certification badges ticket's data model (store under key ksm-66)
  • Apply placeholder badge to the Ashwagandha product page per the Ashwagandha site-fix ticket
  • Verify rendering at all breakpoints — logo must stay above ~60px minimum width even on mobile
  • Test clear-space and alignment against the badges layout
  • Wait for PNG / SVG drop from Monique before production release — swap placeholders for final assets then
  • Once final assets land, verify transparency behaviour on coloured / dark backgrounds
  • Ping Monique for QA once final assets are in place

Notes

This is the first live certification badge on Healios — the rendering you build here becomes the template for future badges (Informed Sport, Vegan Society, etc.) covered in the Certification badges ticket. Build it generically enough that adding future badges is an asset drop + admin flag, not an engineering job.

Issue: Email system ticket — 17-template React Email library for Resend
Issue description:
A complete React Email template library has been built for Healios. Seventeen templates across three categories (transactional, lifecycle, campaign), composed from a modular component system driven by a single design tokens file. Placeholder imagery is in at the correct dimensions; real imagery will replace it once the photo shoot ships.

Goal of this ticket: install the system, wire it into Resend sends from the custom portal, and confirm the dev preview renders correctly across Gmail / Apple Mail / Outlook.

1. Where to get the code

SharePoint link: Emails https://dnthefourths.sharepoint.com/sites/ProjectsServaas/Lists/Issue%20tracker%20list/AllItems.aspx

Zipped folder contains the complete email-system/ directory — tokens.ts, components/ (11 reusable modules), emails/ (17 templates across transactional / lifecycle / campaign), data/sampleProducts.ts, package.json, tsconfig.json, README.md. 50KB compressed, 43 files. No node_modules — fresh install.

2. Setup

bash
unzip healios-email-system.zip
cd email-system
npm install
npm run dev

Opens React Email's dev server at http://localhost:3000 with all 17 templates in a sidebar. Live-reloads on edit. No real send — pure preview.

To export static HTML for any template:

bash
npm run export

Writes HTML files to ./out/.

3. Architecture (read this before touching anything)

Everything cascades from tokens.ts. Change a colour value there and it propagates to every component and every template. Do not hard-code colours, fonts, or spacing inside individual files.

email-system/
├── tokens.ts                  ← design system (colours, fonts, spacing, brand constants)
├── components/                ← 11 reusable modules (Hero, EditorialCard, ProductGrid, etc.)
├── data/sampleProducts.ts     ← preview data — REPLACE with live product data at send time
├── emails/
│   ├── transactional/  (6)    ← order confirm, shipping, delivery, password reset, account created, sub reminder
│   ├── lifecycle/      (6)    ← welcome 1/2/3, abandoned cart, post-purchase, winback
│   └── campaign/       (5)    ← product launch, Wellness Drive, restock, promo, bundle
├── package.json
├── tsconfig.json
└── README.md                  ← full dev documentation

Do not author new HTML. If a template needs a new pattern, build it as a shared component in components/ and import it. Templates are composition, not authoring. The README explains the contribution pattern in full.

4. What you need to do

Phase 1 — local setup and preview (30 min)

  • Unzip, npm install, npm run dev
  • Confirm all 17 templates render in the sidebar
  • Spot-check 3 templates for visual correctness: 01-order-confirmation, 07-welcome-1, 13-product-launch
  • Flag anything that looks visually broken (not placeholder-image related)

Phase 2 — wire into the portal (half a day)

Templates export as React components with typed props. Resend takes them directly via the react field on the send payload. Example pattern (the README has the full version):

ts
import { Resend } from "resend";
import OrderConfirmation from "./emails/transactional/01-order-confirmation";

const resend = new Resend(process.env.RESEND_API_KEY);

await resend.emails.send({
  from: "Healios <hello@thehealios.com>",
  to: customer.email,
  subject: `Order confirmed — ${order.number}`,
  react: OrderConfirmation({
    customerName: customer.firstName,
    orderNumber: order.number,
    items: order.lineItems,   // real data from the order record
    subtotal: formatPrice(order.subtotal),
    shipping: formatPrice(order.shipping),
    total: formatPrice(order.total),
    shippingAddress: { name: ..., lines: [...] },
    trackingUrl: buildTrackingUrl(order),
    unsubscribeUrl: buildUnsubscribeUrl(customer),
    preferencesUrl: buildPreferencesUrl(customer),
  }),
});

What you need to build on the portal side:

  • Hook each trigger event (order placed, order shipped, password reset, etc.) to the correct template
  • Replace data/sampleProducts.ts imports with real product data from the DB
  • Wire unsubscribeUrl and preferencesUrl to the per-customer preference URLs. These MUST be per-recipient at send time — CAN-SPAM and GDPR requirement.

Phase 3 — cross-client QA (half a day)

  • Send one test of each template to your own inbox
  • Verify rendering in: Gmail web + iOS, Apple Mail macOS + iOS, Outlook desktop
  • Outlook's Word-based renderer is the usual source of pain — if anything breaks there, flag and I'll help diagnose
  • Confirm dark-mode doesn't invert awkwardly (the <Head> has color-scheme: light only, but verify)

Phase 4 — handoff

  • Confirm with Monique when setup is complete
  • Real imagery will land as a follow-up (see Section 7 below)

5. Signature typographic move — don't break this

Across every template you'll see an italicWord prop on Hero, EditorialCard, and BigCTA. This renders exactly one word inside the headline in italic Playfair Display — the "Wellness, Elevated." treatment from thehealios.com.

tsx
<Hero
  headline="It's arrived, Monique."
  italicWord="arrived"
/>

If the italicWord isn't found inside the headline string, the headline renders unchanged (safe fallback). This is the single most important typographic detail — it's what ties the email system to the website brand aesthetic. If you need to refactor headline rendering for any reason, preserve this behaviour.

6. Three things that will trip you up if you skip them

  1. Unsubscribe / preferences URLs — every template accepts unsubscribeUrl and preferencesUrl props. Populate these per-recipient at send time. If you omit them, the Footer falls back to {{unsubscribe_url}} / {{preferences_url}} placeholder tokens — useful if Resend substitutes these or you're using merge-variables, but never ship a send without one or the other resolving.
  2. Currency is hard-coded to GBP in the default props. Multi-currency (we ship in 6) needs a currency prop threaded through from the order record. Low priority for v1 — most transactional sends will be UK — but don't forget this when we expand to EU/ZA/AU/CA/US sends.
  3. The italic treatment on the hero word is the brand signature. Don't "clean up" the renderHeadlineWithItalic helper in Hero.tsx / EditorialCard.tsx / BigCTA.tsx — it's deliberately doing a one-word-in-italic render. If you need to change it, ping Monique first.

7. Image assets

Templates currently use placeholder images from placehold.co at the correct dimensions. Monique is commissioning the real image library separately (see Healios-email-image-shot-list.md in the Healios SharePoint folder).

Where to host

Please set up a CDN bucket (Cloudflare R2, CloudFront, or your existing image CDN) scoped to something like cdn.thehealios.com/emails/ and load real images there. Reference by absolute URL from the templates.

Image spec table (full spec in README, short version here)

ModuleRender sizeSupply size (2× for retina)Aspect
Hero600 × 7001200 × 14006:7 portrait
Editorial card backdrop600 × 5001200 × 10006:5
Ingredient callout600 × 6001200 × 12001:1
Closing CTA600 × 4001200 × 8003:2
Product pack shot280 × 280560 × 5601:1
Logo (dark & light)160 × 40320 × 80—
Social icon24 × 2448 × 481:1

Format: JPG for photography, transparent PNG for logos/icons. Weight: under 200KB each, ideally under 100KB. Colour profile: sRGB.

How to swap in real images

  • Global assets (logos, social icons): update tokens.ts — assets.logoDark, assets.logoLight, assets.iconInstagram, etc.
  • Per-template imagery: pass the imageUrl prop at send time, or update the default prop inside the template file.

8. Placeholder values that need real data before go-live

These are currently demo values used so the preview renders without a runtime. Each one needs to be either a) replaced in tokens.ts directly (global), or b) overridden per-send via template props (per-email).

WhereCurrentNeeds to be
tokens.ts → brand.legalAddressPlaceholder London addressReal Healios Ltd registered address
tokens.ts → brand.supportEmailhello@thehealios.comConfirm this is the correct support inbox
components/Footer.tsx → social hrefsinstagram.com/healios etc.Real Healios social handles
data/sampleProducts.tsDemo product dataLive product data from the DB
Welcome / winback / promo discount codes (WELCOME10, BACK15, SPRING20)ExamplesReal codes, set per-campaign

9. Housekeeping

There are nine .tsx.bak files in emails/campaign/ and emails/lifecycle/ — leftover from an import-cleanup pass. They're inert (React Email only discovers .tsx, not .tsx.bak), but safe to delete for tidiness:

bash
find emails -name "*.bak" -delete

The zip delivered via SharePoint already has these excluded — so if you're working from the zip, ignore this note.

Checklist

  • Download zip from SharePoint, unzip
  • npm install, npm run dev — verify preview server starts
  • Verify all 17 templates render in sidebar
  • Wire templates into Resend sending layer in the portal
  • Replace data/sampleProducts.ts imports with live DB data
  • Populate unsubscribeUrl and preferencesUrl per-recipient
  • Update tokens.ts placeholder values (legal address, support email, social hrefs)
  • Set up CDN for email imagery (cdn.thehealios.com/emails/ or similar)
  • Cross-client QA: Gmail, Apple Mail, Outlook (web, iOS, macOS, desktop)
  • Confirm to Monique when complete
  • Real imagery handoff follows separately via Healios-email-image-shot-list.md

Why this matters

Healios doesn't have an email system today. Transactional sends (order confirmation, shipping) are either missing or relying on whatever default Shopify/Stripe/portal templates exist — which don't match the brand. Without a real email system:

  • Transactional emails are the most-opened brand touchpoints we own. Every order confirmation is a chance to reinforce the brand voice and set up the post-purchase journey. Right now we're wasting those touchpoints.
  • Welcome and winback flows are the highest-ROI email campaigns in ecommerce. We can't run them until the infrastructure is in place.
  • The portal already has the trigger hooks (order events, auth events, subscription events). This ticket is about connecting those hooks to real branded templates.

Issue: NEW LAUNCH-CRITICAL TICKET — GDPR-appropriate privacy policy, cookie consent banner, and data-subject request flow
Issue description:
Description

  • Context: Lawyer engagement is deferred. For launch, we follow a standard GDPR-appropriate privacy policy + cookie consent + data-subject-request setup — enough to be defensible, not reinventing the wheel. Every reputable supplement / DTC brand does this; we use an off-the-shelf template and adapt, rather than drafting from scratch.
  • Current behaviour: No published privacy policy. No cookie consent banner. No data-subject-request (DSR) mechanism.
  • Expected behaviour:
    • Privacy policy generated from a reputable multi-regime template (Termly, iubenda, or similar) covering UK GDPR + EU GDPR + POPIA as the baseline. Published at /privacy (or equivalent) and linked from every page footer, checkout, account creation, and email signatures.
    • Policy explicitly covers: what we collect (IPs, behavioural data, order info, email, etc.), lawful basis for each, retention periods, third-party processors (Cloudflare, Stripe, email provider, etc.), cross-border transfers, user rights (access, deletion, portability, objection), cookie use.
    • Cookie consent banner — geolocation-aware. EU / UK / SA visitors get strict opt-in with granular toggles (necessary / analytics / marketing). Other regions get a lighter banner. Consent state persisted.
    • Data-subject request (DSR) mechanism — simple email address or form at /privacy-request (or in account settings) that routes to Monique / a designated privacy contact. DSRs must be actionable within 30 days (GDPR timeline).
    • Terms of Service + cookie policy published alongside the privacy policy.
  • How to test / acceptance criteria:
    • Privacy policy live at /privacy, linked from footer + checkout + account flows + transactional emails
    • Cookie banner shows on first visit; consent state persists; granular toggles function
    • Analytics / tracking respects consent state (don't fire analytics if user declined)
    • DSR mechanism reachable and tested end-to-end (submit a test request, confirm it arrives at the inbox / form destination)
    • Policy coverage verified against UK GDPR + EU GDPR + POPIA checklists (use any of the free compliance-check tools)
    • Privacy policy references the IP posture defined in the sibling ticket
For Servaas: Implementation of the consent banner + DSR mechanism + privacy-policy route.

Issue: NEW LAUNCH-CRITICAL TICKET — IP storage posture, hashing, and retention across the stack
Issue description:
Description

Context: Because we're shipping to UK / EU / SA customers, IP addresses count as personal data under UK GDPR, EU GDPR, and POPIA. We need a defined posture for how IPs are captured, stored, hashed, retained, and purged. This unblocks B5 (analyze popup), B9 (IP on user profile), B10 (audit log IP column), and the internal per-user profile ticket. Without a defined posture, we ship features that create legal exposure.
Current behaviour: Localization middleware uses IP for currency detection; unclear whether IP is stored anywhere queryable. No documented retention period. No hashing strategy. No purge routine. No admin policy on who can see raw IP.
Expected behaviour:

Raw IP capture allowed for short-term security and fraud-prevention window only — recommend 30–90 day rolling retention, configurable.
Hashed IP (one-way, salted SHA-256 or equivalent) stored for long-term analytics — this is what feeds B5, B9, B10, and the internal profile.
Derived region (country, optionally country + city) stored alongside the hashed IP for geographic analytics without exposing the raw address.
Admin surfaces (audit log, internal profile, analyze popup) show hashed IP + country by default. A separate, logged "reveal" flow allows raw IP access for specific incident investigations — every reveal is itself logged with actor, reason, timestamp.
Automatic purge job runs daily, deleting raw IPs older than the retention window.
Anonymous-session stitching (from B9) keeps hashed IP linked to user_id once an account is created.
Lawful basis: documented as legitimate interest (fraud prevention + security + product analytics), with balancing test recorded.
All of this must be disclosed in the privacy policy (sibling ticket below).


How to test / acceptance criteria:

New visitor → raw IP captured with timestamp; hashed IP + country derived and stored
Retention window configurable; purge job verifiable (raw IP older than window is gone)
Admin surfaces show hashed IP + country by default; raw-IP reveal requires explicit action and is itself logged
Anonymous session stitches to user_id on account creation; hashed IP carries forward
Documented lawful basis + balancing test sits in a repo /privacy or similar location
Feature parity with B5, B9, B10, and internal profile tickets — all consume hashed IP + country, not raw



For Servaas: This is the foundational privacy-engineering ticket. Every other IP-related feature depends on it. Please scope and size alongside B9 / B10 / internal profile so we don't build three features three different ways.

Issue: Bundle launch workflow — reusable flow for 4 pre-launch bundles
Issue description:
Summary

Four bundles appear in the sitemap but return "Product Not Found" on live (pre-launch). Build a reusable launch workflow so when each bundle goes live, it's a single admin action — not a bespoke engineering job per bundle.

Background

The four pre-launch bundles:

SlugIntended theme
morning-energy-stackEnergy & vitality
evening-wind-down-stackSleep & relaxation
beauty-glow-stackBeauty / skin-hair-nails
immunity-boost-stackImmunity

Business model per the brand spec: curated 3-SKU bundles at up to 20% off the sum-of-parts price. Individual SKUs inside a bundle are ones Healios already sells.

The v1 products.json excludes these via excluded_from_v1.pre_launch_bundles. Bundles should re-enter the JSON as they launch — one at a time, not in a single batch.

What to build

Bundle data shape

A bundle is a product, but with extra fields:

json
{
  "slug": "morning-energy-stack",
  "type": "bundle",
  "components": ["vitamin-d3-gummies", "acv-ginger-gummies", "..."],
  "bundle_discount_pct": 15,
  "price": { "gbp": "..." },
  "...": "all the usual product fields — name, image, description, category, etc."
}

Pricing logic

  • Bundle's price = (sum of component prices) × (1 − bundle_discount_pct / 100), rounded as per site pricing rules
  • Component prices pulled live from the component SKUs — so if an individual SKU's price changes, the bundle's price updates automatically
  • Admin: Monique sets the bundle_discount_pct per bundle (e.g. 15%, 20%)

Admin flow — "Launch this bundle"

When Monique is ready to launch a bundle, she should be able to:

  1. Open the bundle SKU in the admin
  2. Pick 3 (or more) component SKUs from existing live products
  3. Set the bundle discount %
  4. Add hero image, name, description (content fields)
  5. Set launch status to Live
  6. System then: generates the product page, activates the URL, adds the bundle to products.json, makes it show up in search and category filters

Interaction with subscribe-and-save

Per brand spec, Healios offers subscribe-and-save at 15% off, 30-day cycle. Open question for Monique: do bundles stack with subscribe-and-save, or is it one discount at a time? Flag it in the admin so Monique can toggle per bundle. Default: bundles are not stackable with subscribe-and-save (simpler customer story, avoids mis-discounting).

Category / search surfacing

Bundles should:

  • Appear under their theme category (Energy & Vitality, Sleep & Relaxation, etc.)
  • Appear in a dedicated "Bundles" or "Stacks" category filter
  • Be searchable by component SKU name ("shows up when customer searches Vitamin D" if Vitamin D is in the bundle)

Acceptance criteria

  • Define the bundle data model (type: bundle, components array, discount %)
  • Build the admin flow for creating/launching a bundle
  • Implement automatic bundle pricing from component prices × (1 − discount %)
  • Add bundle inclusion/exclusion to the products.json build pipeline
  • Confirm subscribe-and-save interaction (default non-stackable, per-bundle toggle)
  • Add a dedicated "Stacks" filter in catalogue browse
  • Test with morning-energy-stack as the first launch candidate once Monique has the content ready
  • Ping Monique for QA

Notes

Don't launch all four at once — Monique will stagger bundle launches so each gets its own content treatment and marketing moment.

Component list is Monique's call — the 3-SKU picks for each bundle aren't yet decided. The ticket is scoping the workflow, not the specific contents.

Research tracking — the sheet at Healios/Products/List of Products/healios-products-master.xlsx keeps all 4 bundles as Pre-launch rows for research. Don't delete those rows when bundles launch; update their Launch Status field instead so history is retained.

Issue: Free-from icons on product pages
Issue description:
Summary

Product pages should surface dietary / lifestyle attributes as visible icons (e.g. Vegan, Gluten-free, Nut-free). The products.json already carries these as boolean fields — we need the rendering layer and the icon assets.

Background

Customers scanning product pages need to see dietary suitability at a glance rather than reading it buried in the ingredient list. This is standard for the category and expected by the audience Healios targets. The data is already in place in the v1 JSON under suitability.* and will gradually fill in on Coming Soon products as label info arrives.

What to build

Icons needed

10 free-from / suitability icons, driven by these JSON fields:

JSON fieldIcon label
suitability.veganVegan
suitability.vegetarianVegetarian
suitability.gluten_freeGluten-free
suitability.dairy_freeDairy-free
suitability.soy_freeSoy-free
suitability.nut_freeNut-free
suitability.sugar_freeSugar-free
suitability.gmo_freeGMO-free
suitability.halalHalal
suitability.kosherKosher

Rendering rule

  • Show an icon only when its JSON field is true
  • Do not show a "crossed-out" icon when the field is false (clutter)
  • Do not show any icon for null fields (missing data, not confirmed)
  • Arrange icons in a horizontal strip in the benefits band, or a small grid below the CTA

Assets

  • Monique will either commission an icon set or source from a stock library — open question, see "Notes" below.
  • Icon style should be line-weight / monochrome / on-brand (navy or gold per Healios palette). No third-party trademarked icons (e.g. don't use the Vegan Society logo as a generic vegan icon — that's a separate certification, see the certification badges ticket).

Acceptance criteria

  • Decide rendering pattern (horizontal strip vs grid) and mock up on one product page
  • Wire rendering logic to read suitability.* fields from products.json
  • Handle null (no icon) and false (no icon) — only true renders
  • Test with existing 5 populated products — icons should appear correctly for ACV, D3, Probiotic, Ashwagandha, Magnesium
  • Confirm rendering works when new icons flow in with label backfills on the 9 Coming Soon products
  • Ping Monique for QA

Notes

Icon sourcing is a Monique decision — either she supplies a custom icon set or approves a stock library source. Don't block this ticket on assets; stub with placeholder boxes and swap in once supplied.

Distinct from certification badges — a "Vegan" free-from icon is Healios's own claim based on label info. A "Vegan Society Registered" badge is a third-party certification and has its own ticket.

Issue: Free-from icons on product pages
Issue description:
Summary

Product pages should surface dietary / lifestyle attributes as visible icons (e.g. Vegan, Gluten-free, Nut-free). The products.json already carries these as boolean fields — we need the rendering layer and the icon assets.

Background

Customers scanning product pages need to see dietary suitability at a glance rather than reading it buried in the ingredient list. This is standard for the category and expected by the audience Healios targets. The data is already in place in the v1 JSON under suitability.* and will gradually fill in on Coming Soon products as label info arrives.

What to build

Icons needed

10 free-from / suitability icons, driven by these JSON fields:

JSON fieldIcon label
suitability.veganVegan
suitability.vegetarianVegetarian
suitability.gluten_freeGluten-free
suitability.dairy_freeDairy-free
suitability.soy_freeSoy-free
suitability.nut_freeNut-free
suitability.sugar_freeSugar-free
suitability.gmo_freeGMO-free
suitability.halalHalal
suitability.kosherKosher

Rendering rule

  • Show an icon only when its JSON field is true
  • Do not show a "crossed-out" icon when the field is false (clutter)
  • Do not show any icon for null fields (missing data, not confirmed)
  • Arrange icons in a horizontal strip in the benefits band, or a small grid below the CTA

Assets

  • Monique will either commission an icon set or source from a stock library — open question, see "Notes" below.
  • Icon style should be line-weight / monochrome / on-brand (navy or gold per Healios palette). No third-party trademarked icons (e.g. don't use the Vegan Society logo as a generic vegan icon — that's a separate certification, see the certification badges ticket).

Acceptance criteria

  • Decide rendering pattern (horizontal strip vs grid) and mock up on one product page
  • Wire rendering logic to read suitability.* fields from products.json
  • Handle null (no icon) and false (no icon) — only true renders
  • Test with existing 5 populated products — icons should appear correctly for ACV, D3, Probiotic, Ashwagandha, Magnesium
  • Confirm rendering works when new icons flow in with label backfills on the 9 Coming Soon products
  • Ping Monique for QA

Notes

Icon sourcing is a Monique decision — either she supplies a custom icon set or approves a stock library source. Don't block this ticket on assets; stub with placeholder boxes and swap in once supplied.

Distinct from certification badges — a "Vegan" free-from icon is Healios's own claim based on label info. A "Vegan Society Registered" badge is a third-party certification and has its own ticket.

Issue: Move 9 products to "Coming Soon" state pending label info backfill
Issue description:
Type: Site change / Admin config

Priority: High

Assignee: Servaas Steenkamp

Reporter: Monique Steenkamp (The Fourths)

Date raised: 23 April 2026

Related: Capsule/gummy site-fix ticket (22 April); products.json v1 delivery


SUMMARY

9 of the 14 live products on thehealios.com need to move to a "Coming Soon" state. Product pages stay live but are stripped of ingredient and dosage detail until full label documentation is available. Each page will be converted back to a full product page individually as the label docs come in.


BACKGROUND

The new products.json (v1) only covers 5 products — the ones we have complete label info for. The remaining 9 lack supplier documentation for ingredients, dosages, allergens, and suitability. Rather than publish a JSON/dashboard pair that's ingredient-silent for those products, we're temporarily marking them "Coming Soon" until the docs are sourced and the label data is populated.


SCOPE — THE 9 PRODUCTS

- lions-mane-gummies | https://www.thehealios.com/product/lions-mane | Adaptogens | £21.99

- hair-skin-nails-gummies | https://www.thehealios.com/product/hair-skin-nails | Beauty | £18.99

- iron-vitamin-c-gummies | https://www.thehealios.com/product/iron-vit-c | Vitamins & Minerals | £15.99

- kids-multivitamin-gummies | https://www.thehealios.com/product/kids-multi | Vitamins & Minerals | £14.99

- folic-acid-gummies | https://www.thehealios.com/product/folic-acid | Vitamins & Minerals | £12.99

- turmeric-ginger-gummies | https://www.thehealios.com/product/turmeric-ginger | Adaptogens | £17.99

- halo-glow-collagen | https://www.thehealios.com/product/halo-glow | Beauty | £29.99

- sleep-support-gummies | https://www.thehealios.com/product/sleep-support | Sleep & Relaxation | £16.99

- night-magnesium-gummies | https://www.thehealios.com/product/night-magnesium | Sleep & Relaxation | £20.99


WHAT CHANGES ON EACH PAGE

Keep: Product name; Hero image; Category tag; Short description (the "what it does" benefit line); Longer explainer paragraph (Monique will supply per-product; in the interim, use the existing short description as body copy).

Remove: Ingredient list / dosage table; Allergen declaration; Vegan / vegetarian / suitability badges; "Add to Cart" or buy button; Subscription option (if present); Nutrition facts panel.

Replace "Add to Cart" with either a "Coming Soon" badge in the CTA slot, or a "Notify me when available" email capture form — pick whichever is faster to wire into the existing templates.


FILTER & SEARCH BEHAVIOUR

- These 9 products stay in the catalogue and remain filterable by category (Adaptogens, Beauty, etc.)

- They should not surface in Format / Vegan / Kids / goal-tag filters — the metadata isn't there yet

- Suggested pulling logic: treat status: "coming_soon" as a flag that excludes the product from anything beyond category-level filtering


PAIRED DATA — PRODUCTS.JSON

The v1 products.json (delivered separately) lists these 9 slugs in excluded_from_v1.coming_soon — that array is the authoritative list to key your filter/search logic off.


WHEN LABEL DOCS LAND

Monique will drop new label docs into SharePoint one product at a time. Each time, she'll confirm with you that the product page can be converted back to a full product page (ingredients reinstated, suitability badges, add-to-cart restored, full filter indexing enabled). Expect these in waves, not all at once.


ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

- Add status: "coming_soon" (or equivalent flag) to all 9 products in the admin

- Replace "Add to Cart" with "Coming Soon" badge or "Notify me" form

- Hide ingredient / nutrition / suitability sections on those 9 product pages

- Retain category filter membership; exclude from detail-level filters

- Confirm a single-SKU conversion workflow exists for re-enabling a product once its label info arrives

- Ping Monique when the 9 pages are in Coming Soon state so she can QA


NOTES

Revenue impact: Moving 9 products off active sale is a revenue hit while label info is being sourced. This is an intentional call — better to show incomplete-but-honest product info than ship a dashboard that's ingredient-silent.

Future-proofing: Build the conversion workflow so re-enabling a product is a single-SKU admin action, not a code deploy. Expect to run this 9 times over the next few weeks.

Issue: Product pages — Default the purchase option to 'Subscribe & Save' (not One-time purchase)
Issue description:
SEVERITY: Medium — commercial priority


SUMMARY: On every product page, the purchase selector currently defaults to 'One-time purchase'. Business goal is to grow subscription LTV, so the default option should be 'Subscribe & Save' with the discount visibly applied in the price.


CURRENT BEHAVIOUR: PDP loads with 'One-time purchase' radio pre-selected. User has to actively switch to Subscribe & Save to see the subscription price.


EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR:

- PDP loads with 'Subscribe & Save' pre-selected by default.

- Displayed price reflects the subscriber price (with original price shown as strikethrough).

- Subscription frequency defaults to the most common interval (confirm with Monique: likely 'Every 30 days').

- 'One-time purchase' remains available as the second option for customers who don't want to subscribe.

- Add to cart, from default state, adds the subscription line item.


ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

- Open any product page fresh — Subscribe & Save is the selected option.

- Subscription frequency is pre-filled.

- Subscriber price (discounted) is the price shown above the Add to Cart button.

- Switching to One-time purchase updates the price to full RRP.

- Works across all product types (gummies, capsules, powders).


AFFECTED: All product detail pages across thehealios.com.


NOTE: Confirm with Healios commercial team that the default subscription frequency should be 30 days before shipping.

Issue: Header nav — Search and Wishlist panels must be mutually exclusive (close one when opening the other)
Issue description:
SEVERITY: Medium


SUMMARY: When the Search panel is opened, the Wishlist panel (if open) should close, and vice versa. Currently both can be open simultaneously, which creates overlapping UI and broken focus states.


CURRENT BEHAVIOUR: Opening Search does not close an already-open Wishlist drawer, and opening Wishlist does not close an already-open Search drawer.


EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR:

- Only one header panel can be open at any time.

- Opening Search closes Wishlist (and any other header panel).

- Opening Wishlist closes Search (and any other header panel).

- Escape key and overlay click close whichever panel is open.


ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

- Open Search, then click Wishlist — Search closes before Wishlist opens.

- Open Wishlist, then click Search — Wishlist closes before Search opens.

- Only one panel visible at a time on desktop and mobile.

- Focus is moved into the newly opened panel for accessibility.


AFFECTED: Global header (all pages).

Issue: Product listings — Remove redundant 'Healios' prefix from product names
Issue description:
SEVERITY: Medium — cosmetic but hurts readability, hierarchy and SEO snippets.


SUMMARY: Every product card and product page title on the site starts with "Healios" (e.g. "Healios Magnesium Gummies", "Healios Vitamin D3"). Because the shopper is already on thehealios.com, the brand prefix is redundant, lengthens titles, truncates card names, and weakens the actual product keyword in meta titles.


CURRENT BEHAVIOUR:

- Product card titles: "Healios [Product name]".

- PDP H1 / title tag: "Healios [Product name] | Healios".

- Breadcrumbs and wishlist also repeat "Healios".


EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR:

1. Remove the "Healios" prefix from the displayed product name across cards, PDP H1, breadcrumbs and wishlist.

2. Keep the brand in the title tag suffix only once (e.g. "Magnesium Gummies | Healios") for SEO.

3. Keep "Healios" in structured data (schema.org Product > brand) — just not in the visible name.

4. Bulk-update product records in the CMS rather than styling-hide it, so feeds (Google Shopping, Instagram) inherit the clean name.


ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

- No product on /category/all shows the word "Healios" before its product name.

- PDP H1 reads "Magnesium Gummies", not "Healios Magnesium Gummies".

- Title tag pattern = "[Product] | Healios".

- Shopping feed + sitemap regenerate without regression.

- Old URLs continue to resolve (slugs stay the same).


AFFECTED: Product catalogue (all SKUs), category pages, PDP, wishlist, breadcrumbs, structured data, product feed.

Issue: Shop filters — BUG: Category filter returns 0 products for valid categories
Issue description:
SEVERITY: High — users who filter the shop see an empty state and leave.


SUMMARY: On /category/all, applying a Category filter (e.g., a specific supplement category that clearly has products available in the catalogue) returns 0 products. This is a breaking bug in the shop browse experience — users who try to narrow down by category hit a dead end and bounce.


STEPS TO REPRODUCE:

1. Navigate to https://thehealios.com/category/all.

2. Open the Category filter.

3. Select any category that should contain products.

4. Observe: product grid shows 0 results even though products tagged with that category exist.


EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR: Selecting a category returns all products tagged with that category. If zero genuine matches, show a clear empty state with a "Clear filters" action.


ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS (please verify): likely a mismatch between the category taxonomy value stored on the product records and the filter query parameter (slug vs. display name, case sensitivity, or a whitespace/encoding issue).


ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

1. Selecting a category with tagged products returns those products.

2. Filter combinations (category + price, category + benefit) work correctly.

3. Empty states are only shown when the query genuinely has no matches.

4. Add a unit/integration test covering the filter query path to prevent regression.


AFFECTED: /category/all, Shop browse, all category filter optio

Issue: Checkout — Form validation must highlight empty/invalid required fields on submit attempt
Issue description:
SUMMARY: When a user clicks the primary CTA on the checkout form with required fields empty or invalid, the form does not clearly highlight WHICH fields are the problem. Users see nothing happen (or only a generic error) and have to manually scan the form to figure out what they missed. This is a common drop-off point.


CURRENT BEHAVIOUR: Pressing submit with missing fields either: does nothing visible, scrolls silently, or shows a generic error message — without a red border, inline error copy, or focus shift on the offending field(s).


EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR: On submit attempt with invalid form state:

- All empty required fields get a red border and inline error message (e.g., "This field is required").

- Invalid-format fields (bad email, wrong postal code length, invalid card) also get red border + specific error ("Enter a valid email address").

- Page scrolls/focuses the first invalid field into view.

- Inline errors clear as the user corrects each field (no need to resubmit to clear).


ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

1. All required fields visually indicate error state on failed submit.

2. Each error has specific inline copy (not just a generic banner).

3. First invalid field is focused and scrolled into view.

4. Errors clear on valid input without resubmit.

5. Screen reader users get aria-live announcement of errors.

6. Works on mobile viewport (errors visible, focused field is above keyboard).


  • AFFECTED: /checkout all steps (shipping, billing, paymen

EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR: Display "Powered by Stripe" (or equivalent Stripe badge) plus the relevant card network logos near the payment method section of checkout. Also consider a small lock icon + "Secure checkout" label near the card input fields.


ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

1. Stripe badge visible on checkout page (payment step specifically).

2. Visa + Mastercard logos shown at minimum; add Amex / Apple Pay / Google Pay if enabled in Stripe.

3. Logos use Stripe's official brand assets at correct sizing — do not stretch or recolour.

4. Badge placement does not push primary CTA below the fold on mobile.

5. Do not add badges that imply payment methods we do not actually support.


AFFECTED: /checkout payment step, mobile + desktop.

Issue: Cart — "Shipping calculated at checkout" text is too small (increase from ~10px to 13px)

Issue: Header — ZAR currency dropdown: fix spacing between flag and code
Issue description:
SUMMARY: In the header currency selector, the ZAR option renders with insufficient spacing between the South African flag and the "ZAR" text — the flag and the letters visually collide. The other currencies (GBP, USD, EUR) have correct spacing. Only ZAR is affected.


CURRENT BEHAVIOUR: Flag icon sits flush against the "ZAR" text with no gap (approximately 0-1px). Looks cramped and inconsistent with the other options in the same dropdown list.


EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR: 8px gap between flag and currency code, matching GBP/USD/EUR rows. Consistent vertical alignment across all options.


ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

1. ZAR option has the same flag-to-text gap as other currencies.

2. Vertical centring of flag and text is consistent.

3. Hover state and selected state both render correctly.

4. Verify on desktop and mobile dropdown views.


LIKELY CAUSE: Either the ZAR flag SVG has a different intrinsic width/padding to the other flags, or a specific CSS rule is overriding the default gap for the ZA option.


AFFECTED PAGES: Global header on every page (dropdown appears on hover/click of currency switcher).

Issue: Shop — CRITICAL BUG: All product cards link to Magnesium Gummies PDP (wrong product routing)
Issue description:
SEVERITY: Critical — blocks all revenue on non-Magnesium SKUs.


SUMMARY: On the Shop/All Products page (https://thehealios.com/category/all), every product card routes to the Magnesium Gummies PDP, regardless of which product image is clicked. Customers cannot reach Vitamin D Gummies, Turmeric Gummies, Ashwagandha Gummies, etc. All traffic funnels to one SKU.


CURRENT BEHAVIOUR: Click any product card on the category listing → navigates to /product/magnesium-gummies. Card image, title, and price display correctly per product, but href/onClick is shared/incorrect.


EXPECTED BEHAVIOUR: Each product card must navigate to its own PDP using the correct product slug. Confirm across all listings: /category/all, /category/sleep, /category/focus, /category/beauty, featured collections on homepage, upsell/cross-sell modules.


LIKELY CAUSE: Shared component state or static href. Likely a mapping bug where the slug variable is hardcoded or the first product's slug is reused across the loop.


ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

1. Every card on every listing page routes to the correct PDP.

2. URL slug matches the product displayed on the card.

3. Back-button returns user to the listing with scroll position preserved.

4. Test across desktop, tablet, mobile.


AFFECTED: All SKUs except Magnesium Gummies are currently unreachable from browse flows. Revenue impact is immediate.


DEV NOTES: Please deploy hotfix ASAP. Add a regression test that asserts each card's href matches its product slug.

Issue: Cart — Simplify & reposition Add-to-Cart notifications (remove duplicate toast, anchor below cart icon)
Issue description:
SUMMARY: When a product is added to the bag via the quick-add icon on a product card, two toast notifications fire simultaneously in the top-left of the screen: "[Product] added to bag" and "Added to cart". On repeat clicks (item already in cart), the second toast changes to "Updated quantity in cart". This is redundant and exposes internal logic to the user. It also doesn't align with standard e-commerce conventions — ASOS, Amazon, Boots all anchor cart feedback near the cart icon.


CURRENT BEHAVIOUR:

- Click bag icon on a product card → two toasts top-left: "added to bag" + "Added to cart"

- Click again (already in cart) → "added to bag" + "Updated quantity in cart"


DESIRED BEHAVIOUR / ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:

1. Single notification only — remove the second toast entirely.

2. Content: "[Product name] added to bag" — clear, human, product-specific. No internal system language like "Updated quantity in cart".

3. Position: Move the toast to appear beneath the cart icon in the top-right navigation bar — mirrors standard e-commerce behaviour.

4. No quantity update messaging — whether first add or fifth, the user just needs confirmation. Cart count badge already communicates quantity.


AFFECTED PAGES: https://thehealios.com/category/all and all product listing/category pages where the quick-add bag icon is used.


DEV NOTES:

- Consolidate the two toast triggers into a single event.

- Reposition toast anchor to drop from beneath the cart icon (top-right nav).

- Suppress "Updated quantity in cart" entirely.

(3) After signing in or creating an account, redirect user back to the original product page with the wishlist item automatically saved. (4) The nav user icon should also follow the full sign-in or create account flow. Pages affected: all product listing and product detail pages.
